message:
fix(fcp-sandbox/windows): fail-closed AppContainer availability stub (resolves br-3hrw3)

Pre-fix, `check_appcontainer_available()` at windows.rs:583 returned
`true` unconditionally on the assumption that AppContainer was present
on Windows 8+. That was a stub: the downstream `WindowsSandbox::apply()`
path never invokes any AppContainer code (no `CreateProcessAsUser`, no
AppContainer token — see the inline note at line 351 acknowledging this).
Reporting `true` gave the rest of the system a false sense of process
isolation that the implementation never actually delivered: logs said
"AppContainer available for process isolation" while only job objects +
integrity-level + firewall enforcement was active.

Choice (b) per the bead: flip the stub to fail-closed and require
explicit operator opt-in via the env var
`FCP_SANDBOX_WINDOWS_APPCONTAINER` (now exposed as a public const).
Choice (a) — wiring the real CreateProcessAsUser-based AppContainer
path — is out of scope for the 90-min SLA and would require new
process-creation attributes plus an STARTUPINFOEX integration; that
work belongs in a dedicated bead. Once it lands, the env var stays
the operator opt-in surface — no second stub-flip required.

Add `WindowsSandbox::appcontainer_active()` getter so observability
surfaces and conformance tests can assert the truth (pre-fix the field
was private and no caller could detect the lie). Update the
constructor's info!/warn! log to match the new semantics.

Two regression tests (Windows-only via cfg(target_os = "windows")):
  * appcontainer_inactive_by_default: pins the fail-closed default.
  * appcontainer_active_when_env_opt_in_set: pins that the env opt-in
    surface flips the flag without re-rolling the stub.

cargo test -p fcp-sandbox: 568/569 lib tests pass; the 1 failure
(wasi::tests::test_wasi_runtime_rejects_missing_writable_file_like_preopen)
is pre-existing and unrelated to this change (no edit to wasi.rs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
